title: Bitcoin Price Prediction as BTC Breaks Above $80,000
author: Sigma Capital
metaDescription: Bitcoin has broken above $80,000, with strong short liquidations and bullish buying pressure. It is now targeting $85,000 and beyond. Market optimism is high.
excerpt: Bitcoin has broken above $80,000, with strong short liquidations and bullish buying pressure. It is now targeting $85,000 and beyond. Market optimism is high.

content.introduction: Bitcoin has broken above $80,000, with strong short liquidations and bullish buying pressure. It is now targeting $85,000 and beyond. Market optimism is high.
body: Bitcoin (BTC) has gone up by 15% in the past 30 days, and today’s price action could be paving the way for an even stronger rally toward the $85,000 area. Total **short liquidations spiked to $220 million** in the past 24 hours, with BTC positions accounting for over half of that amount, as the token just broke through a long-dated resistance once again. Oil is still trading above $100, but market participants appear to be expecting that negotiations between the United States and Iran will result in the end of the blockade. Warsh is a bit of a wild card for the market, and that explains why prices have been in consolidation mode ahead of the beginning of his tenure. If the odds of a rate cut this year increase at some point, that would be favorable for Bitcoin (BTC) and other risky assets. Trading volumes are still quite thin, but liquidations point to a significant volume of short positions sitting on top of BTC’s long-dated resistance of $80,000.
Bitcoin is hovering near a critical inflection point as the $70000 support holds firm, keeping hopes alive for a potential breakout toward the $80000 level.

# Bitcoin Price Prediction as BTC Hits $78K Again: Can It Clear $80K? Bitcoin has reclaimed $78,000 after Strategy’s $2.54 billion Bitcoin purchase added bullish buying pressure and Trump’s indefinite ceasefire extension eased macro pressure off the market. Clearing $80,000 would put Bitcoin back above the level it lost in February before the $60K crash, and the 200-day EMA around $83,000 is the next key resistance to target, which would confirm the October-to-February downtrend is over. Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC) is back at the $78,000 price mark five days after last Friday's rally to the same level. The Bitcoin price rallied over 7% last week but Iran’s swift closure of the Strait of Hormuz after a brief 24 hour opening last weekend caused oil prices to spike again. That rattled the market and BTC dropped below $75,000 but has now bounced back to retest $78K. The Bitcoin price hit $78,000 following news that Iran had reopened the Strait of Hormuz, but the move had no real buyers behind it.
